description: Tried to benchmark current working tree in ../coding-agents-plugins, but ./autoresearch.sh was not executable (permission denied).

asi.hypothesis: Use the existing harness to benchmark the current working tree before further optimization.
asi.rollback_reason: Benchmark command failed immediately because the harness lacks execute permission in this repo clone.
asi.next_action_hint: Invoke the harness via bash autoresearch.sh so experiments can proceed without changing file permissions.

description: Baseline for built octocode-cli lightweight startup flows (--help, search-code --help, --version)

asi.hypothesis: Establish a baseline for representative built CLI help/version invocations before optimizing startup imports.
asi.learned: Even no-network help/version paths cost ~180-227ms each, suggesting startup/import overhead dominates more than command logic.

description: Code-split the built CLI and lazy-load commands/tool/help modules so lightweight help/version flows avoid eager startup of the full command graph

asi.hypothesis: The main entrypoint eagerly imports heavy command and tool modules; code splitting plus lazy imports should cut no-network startup time without changing output.
asi.learned: Single-file bundling was the dominant cost. Splitting shrank the entry bundle from ~2.8MB to ~4KB and made general help/version dramatically faster, while command-specific help remained dominated by loading the commands chunk.

description: Split agent subcommand metadata from heavy command handlers so search-code --help and agent command dispatch avoid loading the full commands chunk

asi.hypothesis: Agent subcommands should not need the full admin command registry just to print help or dispatch execution; a tiny metadata/spec chunk can serve those paths.
asi.learned: `search-code --help` was mostly paying for loading `commands.ts`. Moving agent command specs into their own small chunk cut command-help latency by ~4x, and overall startup fell to ~146ms total.

description: Stop preloading agent subcommand metadata on every invocation by keeping the agent-name fast path local and only importing specs when needed

asi.hypothesis: Even a tiny shared metadata chunk can add avoidable startup overhead when it becomes a hard dependency of the entrypoint.
asi.learned: Removing the eager shared agent-spec dependency shaved another ~16ms total and brought general help back near the earlier low-latency result while preserving fast agent command help.

description: Tried replacing the shared colors import in src/index.ts with a local dim helper to avoid one startup dependency, but runtime regressed badly

asi.hypothesis: Eliminating a tiny shared colors chunk from the entrypoint might shave a bit more startup overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: The bundle shape changed in a way that materially regressed all benchmarked paths, so this is not a profitable micro-optimization.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the split/lazy-loading architecture and avoid touching tiny shared utilities unless bundle output shows a concrete startup win.
asi.learned: Seemingly small source edits can perturb esbuild chunking enough to destroy the startup gains; inspect the emitted graph before retrying low-level bundle tweaks.

description: Tried top-level argv preflight in src/index.ts to short-circuit pure --help/--version before loading cli/index, but it did not beat the current best

asi.hypothesis: A tiny entrypoint preflight for bare --help/--version could avoid loading parser/CLI glue and improve startup further.
asi.rollback_reason: The extra branching and alternate entry flow underperformed the current best total_ms, so it is not worth keeping.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat the current ~130ms result as near the floor unless a larger structural change appears; avoid more top-level micro-routing tweaks.
asi.learned: After code splitting, top-level routing overhead is already small; additional preflight logic mostly adds noise and branch cost instead of removing meaningful work.
